root_text.an8.62:1.1: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano alaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:1.7: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo, poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagaḷāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an8.62:1.8: sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:1.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano alaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano alaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:2.8: sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:2.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano alaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:3.1: Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano nālaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:3.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:3.7: no ca kalyāṇavāco hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo, poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagaḷāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an8.62:3.8: no ca sandassako hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:3.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano nālaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:4.1: Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ nālaṁ attano.
root_text.an8.62:4.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:4.5: no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti;
root_text.an8.62:4.6: na ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an8.62:4.7: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo …pe… atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an8.62:4.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
root_text.an8.62:5.1: Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano nālaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:5.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:5.7: no ca kalyāṇavāco hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo, poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagaḷāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an8.62:5.8: no ca sandassako hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:5.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:6.1: Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
root_text.an8.62:6.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:6.5: no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti;
root_text.an8.62:6.6: no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an8.62:6.8: sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:6.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
root_text.an8.62:7.1: Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:7.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:7.4: no ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti;
root_text.an8.62:7.7: no ca kalyāṇavāco hoti …pe… atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an8.62:7.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ attano, nālaṁ paresaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:8.1: Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano.
root_text.an8.62:8.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu na heva kho khippanisanti ca hoti kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an8.62:8.4: no ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti;
root_text.an8.62:8.5: no ca dhātānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti;
root_text.an8.62:8.6: no ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti;
root_text.an8.62:8.7: kalyāṇavāco ca hoti kalyāṇavākkaraṇo, poriyā vācāya samannāgato vissaṭṭhāya anelagaḷāya atthassa viññāpaniyā;
root_text.an8.62:8.8: sandassako ca hoti samādapako samuttejako sampahaṁsako sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an8.62:8.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ paresaṁ, nālaṁ attano”ti.

translation_text.an8.62:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with six qualities is good enough for themselves and others.
translation_text.an8.62:1.3: A mendicant is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:1.4: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:1.5: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:1.6: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:1.7: They’re a good speaker who enunciates well. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:1.8: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:1.9: A mendicant with these six qualities is good enough for themselves and others.
translation_text.an8.62:2.1: A mendicant with five qualities is good enough for themselves and others.
translation_text.an8.62:2.3: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:2.4: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:2.5: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:2.6: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:2.7: They’re a good speaker. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:2.8: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:2.9: A mendicant with these five qualities is good enough for themselves and others.
translation_text.an8.62:3.1: A mendicant with four qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.
translation_text.an8.62:3.3: A mendicant is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:3.4: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:3.5: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:3.6: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:3.7: But they’re not a good speaker and do not enunciate well. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:3.8: They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:3.9: A mendicant with these four qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.
translation_text.an8.62:4.1: A mendicant with four qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.
translation_text.an8.62:4.3: A mendicant is quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:4.4: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:4.5: But they don’t examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:4.6: Not understanding the meaning and the teaching, they don’t practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:4.7: They’re a good speaker who enunciates well. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:4.8: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:4.9: A mendicant with these four qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.
translation_text.an8.62:5.1: A mendicant with three qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.
translation_text.an8.62:5.3: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:5.4: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:5.5: They examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:5.6: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:5.7: But they’re not a good speaker and do not enunciate well. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:5.8: They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:5.9: A mendicant with these three qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.
translation_text.an8.62:6.1: A mendicant with three qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.
translation_text.an8.62:6.3: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:6.4: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:6.5: But they don’t examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:6.6: Not understanding the meaning and the teaching, they don’t practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:6.7: They’re a good speaker. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:6.8: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:6.9: A mendicant with these three qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.
translation_text.an8.62:7.1: A mendicant with two qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.
translation_text.an8.62:7.3: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:7.4: And they don’t readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:7.5: But they examine the meaning of teachings they have memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:7.6: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:7.7: They’re not a good speaker. Their voice isn’t polished, clear, articulate, and doesn’t express the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:7.8: They don’t educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:7.9: A mendicant with these two qualities is good enough for themselves but not for others.
translation_text.an8.62:8.1: A mendicant with two qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.
translation_text.an8.62:8.3: A mendicant is not quick-witted when it comes to skillful teachings.
translation_text.an8.62:8.4: And they don’t readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard.
translation_text.an8.62:8.5: Nor do they examine the meaning of teachings they’ve memorized.
translation_text.an8.62:8.6: Not understanding the meaning and the teaching, they don’t practice in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an8.62:8.7: But they’re a good speaker who enunciates well. Their voice is polished, clear, articulate, and expresses the meaning.
translation_text.an8.62:8.8: They educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an8.62:8.9: A mendicant with these two qualities is good enough for others but not for themselves.”

variant_text.an8.62:1.5: dhātānañca → dhatānañca (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | atthūpaparikkhitā → atthupaparikkhī (sya-all, pts1ed)

reference_text.an8.62:1.1: csp1ed17.119, csp2ed17.119, dr23.321, ms17A8_631, msdiv62, pts-vp-pli4.296
